signals.title: NZD/CAD — G8 Bilateral Theme + Energy Shock
signals.text: NZD/CAD spread hits 1.325% as NZD leads G8 performance while CAD lags. Divergence is driven by RBNZ hike bias (2.25% ON HOLD, bias ↑) vs BoC Governor Macklem's warning that energy shocks from the Iran conflict necessitate consecutive hikes to combat inflationary pressure. Despite Macklem's hawkishness, CAD remains the G8 laggard, suggesting market skepticism or focus on broader USD/Iran de-escalation. NZD/USD at 0.5964 and USD/CAD at 1.3635.

signals.title: USD/JPY — Geopolitical De-escalation + Carry
signals.text: USD/JPY at 156.37 as DXY hits 2.5-month lows on hopes for an imminent end to the US-Iran war. Fed 3.75% vs BoJ 0.75% (+300bp) carry sustains the floor, but JPY consolidation persists as intervention fears and a hawkish BoJ bias (↑ Hike) limit upside. JPY COT LF net short -88,543 remains crowded; a sustained break below 156.00 targets the 155.20 support zone if NFP data underwhelms.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Positioning + Retail Divergence
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.7252 supported by a -60bp carry premium (RBA 4.35% vs Fed 3.75%). COT LF net long +48,299 (74.8% long) shows institutional conviction, while retail sentiment is 89% short. This extreme divergence suggests retail is fading the trend; a break above 0.7300 likely triggers retail capitulation and a squeeze toward 0.7450. Watch for RBA hold confirmation at the next meeting.

signals.title: EUR/USD — Correlation Break + US NFP
signals.text: EUR/USD vs EuroStoxx correlation at +0.721 vs norm of -0.145 (z=+1.96σ), signaling a total breakdown of the traditional risk-off EUR/equity inverse relationship. EUR/USD at 1.1756 is trading as a high-beta growth proxy rather than a funding currency. Implication: EUR/USD upside is now tethered to European equity strength rather than just USD weakness. Watch US NFP release for a catalyst to reset or extend this decoupling.

signals.title: NZD/USD — COT vs OIS Divergence
signals.text: NZD/USD COT LF net short -16,833 (long% 23.0%) contradicts the RBNZ hawkish bias (↑ Hike). Specs are heavily positioned for NZD weakness despite the RBNZ signaling further tightening. If the RBNZ reaffirms the hike bias on 27 May, the crowded spec short position faces significant unwind risk. NZD/USD at 0.5964.
